post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 254: Destination FRC

It rains today. You enter the stadium, excitement coursing through your system. The cheers are deafening, but you pay them no mind. You've made it from all sorts of backgrounds, from all sorts of countries and states. Whether you be a small team working long hours to finally be lucky enough to be picked by the top seeds, or the long-term reigning champion, you will meet on field, for honor and for victory. You align your robot into its place, ready your scouting notes, and nervously watch the clock as you wait for the referee to wave his hand down to start the match.
It is time.

Rules
  • Rules
    • 1.1: Understand the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • 1.2: Do not post at night.
    • 1.3: You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
    • 1.4: Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
    • 1.5: As proof that you understand the mechanics by which this game goes by, PM me your alliance's second pick.
    • 1.6: Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • 1.7: If you are not a living participant of the game, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • 1.8: Do not advocate for breaking the rules.
    • 1.9: Do not use the colors red (#FF0000), blue (#0000FF), green (#00FF00), or anything that appears similar, as these colors are used for modding purposes.
  • Voting/Hanging
    • 2.1: Post votes in code tags in the format 
      [code]Vote: <insert player here>[/‎code]

      Since AoPS does not distinguish between usernames with different capitalizations, the capitalization of the player name does not matter. A proper vote looks like 
      Vote: e_is_cool
    • 2.2: Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
    • 2.3: Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until I made the official end of the game post.
    • 2.4: You may vote for no hang to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post 
      Vote: No Hang
    • 2.5: You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format 
      Vote: Unvote

      An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • 2.6: Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
    • 2.7: If at any point in a day more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • 2.8: If at any point in a day at least half of the votes are for no hang, then there will be no hang. It will then be twilight.
    • 2.9: If the deadline is met then the player with the most votes will be hanged. Ties will be broken by the time the most recent vote on a person was made, hanging the person with the least recently applied vote.
    • 2.10: The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
    • 2.11: During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • 2.12: Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.

  • Activity
    • 3.1: Each day will be 336 hours (2 weeks). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
    • 3.2: The deadlines will follow the official time
    • 3.3: The deadline will occur the very second 336 hours has occurred.
    • 3.4: The night deadline will be 72 hours after night begins.
    • 3.5: When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
    • 3.6: Any night results will be sent after the night deadline
    • 3.7: Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night.
    • 3.8: If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, you will receive a pm prod.
    • 3.9: If a player does not post in a 96-hour period then they will be replaced.
    • 3.10: If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.
    • 3.11: Whether a player's post counts for activity is up to the moderator's discretion. Warning: flaking does not count for activity.

  • Etiquette
    • 4.1: Do not use inappropriate language.
    • 4.2: Do not make any personal attacks.
    • 4.3: Follow all of the rules.
    • 4.4: Be mature.
    • 4.5: For a minor rule infraction, players can receive up to two warnings. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled or force replaced. Please do not make me modkill you.

  • Moderators/Co-Moderators
    • 5.1: Charge is the moderator of Mafia Game 254 and fdas is the co-mod.
    • 5.2: Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
    • 5.3: Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
    • 5.4: Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
    • 5.5: The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount
    • 5.6: If an error to the votecount is not pointed out within 24 hours, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • 5.7: The moderator may change the rules at any time.
    • 5.8: The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.

Setup
- This game is closed 13 player setup.
- No roles may target themselves unless otherwise specified.
- You may claim your role name, but you may not quote any Role PMs.
- There are no hidden win conditions.
- The moderators do not lie.
- Investigative roles are sane.
- Actions are not compulsive.

Flavor
EoD1
All teams were hard at work at the time of the competition. Tension ran in the air.
Who would be making it the finals? Many were wondering.

Well, one thing was sure. vietaformula810 (Team 179, Children of the Swamp, Vanilla Townie) had a broken robot. They weren't sure exactly which match it happened, or how it happened, but it seemed they'd be out of the running for a while... and probably out of the finals, since they'd taken significant damage. For some reason, no one could spare the parts they needed either. And because they were the first pick, their alliance would suffer greatly. They sat on the sidelines as their alliance was called up for the next match, and hoped their alliance could win the championship with a replaced robot.

EoN1
After a few matches, the Citrus Circuits made their way back to the stands and the pits for scouting and robot maintenance, respectively. They were hard at work, since they knew their next match would be a while. The robot had had some unnatural denting in the last match, so they'd need some time to fix it. They were confident however that they would be back up and running before the next match.

That is, if the next match was as they thought it was.
Someone had switched around their match schedule info. When a scouting team member had noticed their info was wrong when comparing to TBA, they were already too late. They hurried to pull the robot over to the match, but it seemed that they'd already been marked absent from the match. They sighed, and pulled the robot back to the pit, figuring they'd join the next match. In the process, however, someone bumped into one of the drivers for the team, who happened to fall right into the tracks of the moving pit cart.

With the lead driver's hands damaged, and the substitutes not in Houston, they would have a hard time participating in any later matches.
And therefore, aroodman (Team 1678, Citrus Circuits, Vanilla Townie) was out of the game.

EoD2
It was strange.
The team had never made it to Einstein Field to begin with... but they were still there, in the bleachers, in the crowd, in the pits even.

They had had some tough matches against Team 1678, the Citrus Circuits, but despite the loss, the team stayed to watch the final matches. They were near enough that they decided to stay and watch, and perhaps they'd cheer for the alliance that tossed them from Einstein Field. They donated spare parts if any teams needed them, and they'd sit with other competing teams on the fields to chat around about the hype.

What would you expect from the professional FRC team with the assistance of NASA?

Unfortunately, cai40 (Team 118, The Robonauts, Vanilla Townie) was unable to stay for the duration of the event. While some of the team members had been playing around with the Robowranglers, many of the member's personal belongings had been stolen. The robot, in the meantime, had also gone missing. Much of the team went in a rush to ask around about where there stuff had been taken, and slowly their seats in the bleachers got taken up by other teams. Although their competition was over, a lost robot was unheard of. In a flurry of panic, they left the vicinity of Einstein Field.

EoN2
The Greybots were confident that they'd last at least until the finals match. They had MadTown Robotics on their side, and the alliance had practiced quite a bit. And yet... the team could feel that something was amiss.

They found their source of worry soon enough once the match had started. The robot was out of battery, and would not move. The person responsible for keeping track had forgotten about it and would sure get a good talking to. They'd have to at least give up this match and hope their allies would be able to carry them to victory.

Soon enough though, some team members in the pits reported that the batteries were either damaged or had gone missing. What a coincidence...
Either way, it seemed that iamafluffyrabbit (Team 973, Greybots, Vanilla Townie) would have a hard time participating with a working robot in later matches.

EoD3
 

EoN3
 

EoD4
 

Warnings

post_rendered:
All teams were hard at work at the time of the competition. Tension ran in the air.
Who would be making it the finals? Many were wondering.

Well, one thing was sure. vietaformula810 (Team 179, Children of the Swamp, Vanilla Townie) had a broken robot. They weren't sure exactly which match it happened, or how it happened, but it seemed they'd be out of the running for a while... and probably out of the finals, since they'd taken significant damage. For some reason, no one could spare the parts they needed either. And because they were the first pick, their alliance would suffer greatly. They sat on the sidelines as their alliance was called up for the next match, and hoped their alliance could win the championship with a replaced robot.

Day has ended. Night has begun.
post_canonical:
All teams were hard at work at the time of the competition. Tension ran in the air. 
Who would be making it the finals? Many were wondering.

Well, one thing was sure. [color=#0f0]vietaformula810 (Team 179, Children of the Swamp, Vanilla Townie)[/color] had a broken robot. They weren't sure exactly which match it happened, or how it happened, but it seemed they'd be out of the running for a while... and probably out of the finals, since they'd taken significant damage. For some reason, no one could spare the parts they needed either. And because they were the first pick, their alliance would suffer greatly. They sat on the sidelines as their alliance was called up for the next match, and hoped their alliance could win the championship with a replaced robot.

[color=#00f][b]Day has ended. Night has begun.[/b][/color]

post_rendered:
After a few matches, the Citrus Circuits made their way back to the stands and the pits for scouting and robot maintenance, respectively. They were hard at work, since they knew their next match would be a while. The robot had had some unnatural denting in the last match, so they'd need some time to fix it. They were confident however that they would be back up and running before the next match.

That is, if the next match was as they thought it was.
Someone had switched around their match schedule info. When a scouting team member had noticed their info was wrong when comparing to TBA, they were already too late. They hurried to pull the robot over to the match, but it seemed that they'd already been marked absent from the match. They sighed, and pulled the robot back to the pit, figuring they'd join the next match. In the process, however, someone bumped into one of the drivers for the team, who happened to fall right into the tracks of the moving pit cart.

With the lead driver's hands damaged, and the substitutes not in Houston, they would have a hard time participating in any later matches.
And therefore, aroodman (Team 1678, Citrus Circuits, Vanilla Townie) was out of the game.

Night has ended. Day has begun.

post_rendered:
It was strange.
The team had never made it to Einstein Field to begin with... but they were still there, in the bleachers, in the crowd, in the pits even.

They had had some tough matches against Team 1678, the Citrus Circuits, but despite the loss, the team stayed to watch the final matches. They were near enough that they decided to stay and watch, and perhaps they'd cheer for the alliance that tossed them from Einstein Field. They donated spare parts if any teams needed them, and they'd sit with other competing teams on the fields to chat around about the hype.

What would you expect from the professional FRC team with the assistance of NASA?

FLIP
Unfortunately, cai40 (Team 118, The Robonauts, Vanilla Townie) was unable to stay for the duration of the event. While some of the team members had been playing around with the Robowranglers, many of the member's personal belongings had been stolen. The robot, in the meantime, had also gone missing. Much of the team went in a rush to ask around about where there stuff had been taken, and slowly their seats in the bleachers got taken up by other teams. Although their competition was over, a lost robot was unheard of. In a flurry of panic, they left the vicinity of Einstein Field.


Day has ended. Night has begun.
post_canonical:
It was strange.
The team had never made it to Einstein Field to begin with... but they were still there, in the bleachers, in the crowd, in the pits even.

They had had some tough matches against Team 1678, the Citrus Circuits, but despite the loss, the team stayed to watch the final matches. They were near enough that they decided to stay and watch, and perhaps they'd cheer for the alliance that tossed them from Einstein Field. They donated spare parts if any teams needed them, and they'd sit with other competing teams on the fields to chat around about the hype. 

What would you expect from the professional FRC team with the assistance of NASA?

[hide=FLIP]Unfortunately, [color=#0f0]cai40 (Team 118, The Robonauts, Vanilla Townie)[/color] was unable to stay for the duration of the event. While some of the team members had been playing around with the Robowranglers, many of the member's personal belongings had been stolen. The robot, in the meantime, had also gone missing. Much of the team went in a rush to ask around about where there stuff had been taken, and slowly their seats in the bleachers got taken up by other teams. Although their competition was over, a lost robot was unheard of. In a flurry of panic, they left the vicinity of Einstein Field.[/hide]

[color = #00f][b]Day has ended. Night has begun. [/b][/color]

post_rendered:
The Greybots were confident that they'd last at least until the finals match. They had MadTown Robotics on their side, and the alliance had practiced quite a bit. And yet... the team could feel that something was amiss.

They found their source of worry soon enough once the match had started. The robot was out of battery, and would not move. The person responsible for keeping track had forgotten about it and would sure get a good talking to. They'd have to at least give up this match and hope their allies would be able to carry them to victory.

Soon enough though, some team members in the pits reported that the batteries were either damaged or had gone missing. What a coincidence...
Either way, it seemed that iamafluffyrabbit (Team 973, Greybots, Vanilla Townie) would have a hard time participating with a working robot in later matches.

Night has ended. Day has begun.

post_rendered:
They were the first captain in Galileo Division and they'd been able to get to Einstein Field without much worry. Spartan Robotics knew they'd have to face some harder competition in the round robin, but they figured they could push through the rounds without much worry. They'd only have to watch out for this year's well known robots and their alliances: like perhaps Madtown Robotics and The Cheesy Poofs.

Things had gone awry not long ago though, with The Children In the Swamp out early due to their broken robot. As the alliance captain, they had tried to find any spare parts they had to save the alliance from having to use backup robots, but unfortunately, their members found no parts lying around that would be useful to their first pick.

The alliance's performance declined since then. The team kept their hopes up, since they at least still had 3 robots competing as an alliance, but the power difference was too great. Many other alliances had two powerful offence robots, and with the lack of Team 179, the alliance was losing out on firepower. Only something like a miracle or a great misfortune to the other teams would set them back on track.

Unfortunately, as expected, no good news were in place for the team. Osmium_ (Team 971, Spartan Robotics), was soon defeated in the series of round robin matches.

Night has ended. Day has begun.

Flavor TBD
FLIP
Mathletesv (Team 2046, Bear Metal, Vanilla Townie)


---

The field was tense in anticipation.
They watched as MadTown Robotics and their alliance took the field. Would there finally be an upset this year? Would they finally obtain the victory they deserved?

Alas, as autonomous started, the Greybots's robot moved past the planned mark and forced a hatch onto the ground instead of the Velcro on the cargo ship. Such a mishap foreshadowed the strange string of unlucky events that happened afterwards. The crowd became silent, half in despair, as the alliance lost their first match to The Cheesy Poofs's alliance. It would be hard to win now.

In the second round, it seemed that the Greybots's robot had the correct distance... Perhaps they could score some more points now?
Unfortunately, however, the hatch the team had preloaded onto the robot would not stick to the Velcro, and at the end of the 15 seconds, the cargo rolled out. The alliance was behind yet again. With MadTown's and Greybots's combined skills, however, they were able to catch up. Their defense bot also performed excellently to prevent The Cheesy Poofs and Black Hawk Robotics from scoring.

The crowd was on their feet. Perhaps there was a chance for them to win.

They despaired not too long after when they realized the point difference had begun to widen. The match ended close, but a clear win for the Red Alliance, 114 to 103.

Congratulations to falliinqsnow- (Team 254, the Cheesy Poofs) and ad333 (Team 3310, Black Hawk Robotics) as well as their third pick Team 6986, PPT Bots, for winning on Einstein Field in Houston.
